title: System Core — Configuration and Environment
description: Create an AppConfig class that reads from a config dict with sensible defaults for app_name ('CommandCenter'), host ('127.0.0.1'), port (8080), db_path ('data/command_center.db'), log_path ('data/logs/app.log'), and plugin_dir ('plugins'). Provide get(key), set(key, value), and as_dict() methods. Then create a SystemInfo class with methods: get_platform() returning a dict with system/node/release/version/machine, get_memory_usage() returning a dict with rss_bytes, get_disk_usage(path) returning a dict with total/used/free, and get_uptime() returning seconds since the object was created. Use os, sys, and time modules.
starter_code:
# Personal Command Center — Step 1: System Core
import os
import sys
import time
import platform


class AppConfig:
    """Application configuration with sensible defaults.

    Defaults:
        app_name = 'CommandCenter'
        host = '127.0.0.1'
        port = 8080
        db_path = 'data/command_center.db'
        log_path = 'data/logs/app.log'
        plugin_dir = 'plugins'

        'host': '127.0.0.1',
        'port': 8080,

test_code:
# Tests for Step 1: AppConfig and SystemInfo
import os
import sys
import time

assert 'AppConfig' in dir(), 'AppConfig class must be defined'
assert 'SystemInfo' in dir(), 'SystemInfo class must be defined'

# Test AppConfig defaults
config = AppConfig()
assert config.get('app_name') == 'CommandCenter', 'app_name should default to CommandCenter'
assert config.get('host') == '127.0.0.1', 'host should default to 127.0.0.1'
assert config.get('port') == 8080, 'port should default to 8080'
assert config.get('db_path') == 'data/command_center.db', 'db_path default wrong'
assert config.get('log_path') == 'data/logs/app.log', 'log_path default wrong'
assert config.get('plugin_dir') == 'plugins', 'plugin_dir default wrong'

# Test AppConfig get with missing key
assert config.get('nonexistent') is None, 'Missing key should return None'
assert config.get('nonexistent', 'fb') == 'fb', 'Missing key should return given default'

# Test AppConfig set
config.set('host', '0.0.0.0')
assert config.get('host') == '0.0.0.0', 'set() should update the value'

# Test AppConfig overrides
config2 = AppConfig({'port': 9090, 'app_name': 'TestApp'})
assert config2.get('port') == 9090, 'Override should take effect'
assert config2.get('app_name') == 'TestApp', 'Override should take effect'
assert config2.get('host') == '127.0.0.1', 'Non-overridden key keeps default'

# Test AppConfig as_dict
d = config2.as_dict()
assert isinstance(d, dict), 'as_dict must return a dict'
assert d['port'] == 9090, 'as_dict should reflect current values'

# Test SystemInfo get_platform
info = SystemInfo()
plat = info.get_platform()
assert isinstance(plat, dict), 'get_platform must return a dict'
assert 'system' in plat, 'get_platform must include system'
assert 'node' in plat, 'get_platform must include node'
assert 'machine' in plat, 'get_platform must include machine'

# Test SystemInfo get_memory_usage
mem = info.get_memory_usage()
assert isinstance(mem, dict), 'get_memory_usage must return a dict'
assert 'rss_bytes' in mem, 'get_memory_usage must include rss_bytes'
assert isinstance(mem['rss_bytes'], int), 'rss_bytes must be an int'
assert mem['rss_bytes'] > 0, 'rss_bytes must be positive'

# Test SystemInfo get_disk_usage
disk = info.get_disk_usage('.')
assert isinstance(disk, dict), 'get_disk_usage must return a dict'
assert 'total' in disk and 'used' in disk and 'free' in disk, 'get_disk_usage must have total/used/free'
assert disk['total'] > 0, 'total disk must be positive'

# Test SystemInfo get_uptime
time.sleep(0.1)
uptime = info.get_uptime()
assert isinstance(uptime, (int, float)), 'get_uptime must return a number'
assert uptime >= 0.1, 'get_uptime should reflect elapsed time'
acceptance_criteria: AppConfig class is defined with DEFAULTS dict containing all 6 keys
AppConfig constructor accepts optional overrides dict
get(key, default=None) returns config value or default
as_dict() returns a copy of the full configuration
SystemInfo.get_memory_usage() returns dict with rss_bytes as positive int
hints: Start with self._config = dict(self.DEFAULTS) to copy defaults, then update with overrides.
Use platform.uname() to get system, node, release, version, machine in one call.
shutil.disk_usage(path) returns a named tuple with total, used, free — works cross-platform.
For memory, try: import resource; resource.getrusage(resource.RUSAGE_SELF).ru_maxrss, or just use os.getpid() and return a reasonable estimate.
